description:
Goal: World::new should allocate stable runtime module identities and compiler-owned prelude code without lexing or parsing runtime source. Signal: Compiler2 quicksort telemetry reports zero pre-drive lexer/parser work, while quicksort and Enum.reduce still pull referenced runtime modules through ordinary Compiler2 jobs. Strategy: runtime bootstrap records module ids and source text only; `runtime.fz` is parsed by a normal source job when scoped code needs the prelude namespace, and runtime module source enters the same Code/Index/Scope/Define path as user source when demanded.

Change:
- Remove eager runtime lex/parse from Compiler2 construction.
- Treat `runtime.fz` as compiler-owned source whose scoped namespace becomes the prelude.
- Let exact `only:` imports bind MFA references without warming provider modules.
- Pull runtime source, protocol callbacks, imports, and impl surfaces through ordinary Compiler2 jobs on first demand.

Why:
- Runtime work before the first drive violates the pull-based model and hides real compile cost outside the work graph.

Checks:
- Quicksort pre-drive telemetry reports zero frontend work.
- Runtime-backed quicksort and Enum.reduce scenarios still settle through ordinary Compiler2 jobs.
- A root that never reaches runtime names keeps runtime modules cold.
title: Compiler2 runtime bootstrap: replace eager parsing with lazy surface bootstrap
